description: Reference regional intelligence snapshot — Pike County, PA (default). Same shape WDS.regionalIntelligence.get() returns.

geography.elevationNote: County mean ~1,200 ft; Delaware River valley ~400 ft to plateau balds ~1,800 ft
geography.bioregion: Northeastern Pennsylvania · Pocono Plateau · Delaware River Highlands
geography.dominantForest: Northern hardwoods with oak-hickory on dry ridges; hemlock–northern hardwood ravines along Delaware tributaries

phenology.stage: Late spring transition — ephemerals fading, mountain laurel opening, morels ending below 1,200 ft

weather.summary: 74°F / 52°F · Humid · valley fog at dawn · thunderstorm chance Saturday after 3 p.m.
weather.conditions: Humid · valley fog at dawn · thunderstorm chance Saturday after 3 p.m.

recent.summary: Thursday rain elevated creek levels; ravines muddy, rocky ridges drying fast

active.name: Mountain laurel
active.note: North slopes and ravines 800–1,400 ft — peak next week

active.name: Whitetail antler growth
active.note: Bucks in velvet — observe at field edges at dawn

active.name: Yellow morel
active.note: River bottoms and old orchards below 1,200 ft — ending soon
